root_text.an10.10:1.1: “Saddho ca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu hoti, no ca sīlavā.
root_text.an10.10:1.5: Yato ca kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu saddho ca hoti, sīlavā ca, evaṁ so tenaṅgena paripūro hoti.
root_text.an10.10:2.1: Saddho ca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu hoti sīlavā ca, no ca bahussuto bahussuto ca,
root_text.an10.10:2.3: dhammakathiko ca, no ca parisāvacaro parisāvacaro ca,
root_text.an10.10:2.4: no ca visārado parisāya dhammaṁ deseti visārado ca parisāya dhammaṁ deseti,
root_text.an10.10:2.6: no ca anekavihitaṁ pubbenivāsaṁ anussarati, seyyathidaṁ—ekampi jātiṁ dvepi jātiyo …pe… iti sākāraṁ sauddesaṁ anekavihitaṁ pubbenivāsaṁ anussarati. Anekavihitañca …pe… pubbenivāsaṁ anussarati,
root_text.an10.10:2.7: no ca dibbena cakkhunā visuddhena atikkantamānusakena …pe… yathākammūpage satte pajānāti dibbena ca cakkhunā visuddhena atikkantamānusakena …pe…
root_text.an10.10:2.8: yathākammūpage satte pajānāti, no ca āsavānaṁ khayā …pe… sacchikatvā upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an10.10:2.11: ‘kintāhaṁ saddho ca assaṁ, sīlavā ca, bahussuto ca, dhammakathiko ca, parisāvacaro ca, visārado ca parisāya dhammaṁ deseyyaṁ, vinayadharo ca, anekavihitañca pubbenivāsaṁ anussareyyaṁ, seyyathidaṁ—ekampi jātiṁ dvepi jātiyo …pe… iti sākāraṁ sauddesaṁ anekavihitaṁ pubbenivāsaṁ anussareyyaṁ, dibbena ca cakkhunā visuddhena atikkantamānusakena …pe… yathākammūpage satte pajāneyyaṁ, āsavānañca khayā …pe… sacchikatvā upasampajja vihareyyan’ti.
root_text.an10.10:3.1: Yato ca kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu saddho ca hoti, sīlavā ca, bahussuto ca, dhammakathiko ca, parisāvacaro ca, visārado ca parisāya dhammaṁ deseti, vinayadharo ca, anekavihitañca pubbenivāsaṁ anussarati, seyyathidaṁ—ekampi jātiṁ dvepi jātiyo …pe… iti sākāraṁ sauddesaṁ anekavihitaṁ pubbenivāsaṁ anussarati, dibbena ca cakkhunā visuddhena atikkantamānusakena …pe… yathākammūpage satte pajānāti, āsavānañca khayā anāsavaṁ cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ diṭṭheva dhamme sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an10.10:3.3: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, dasahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu samantapāsādiko ca hoti sabbākāraparipūro cā”ti.

translation_text.an10.10:0.3: Inspiring All Around: the Three Knowledges
translation_text.an10.10:1.1: “A mendicant is faithful, but not ethical.
translation_text.an10.10:1.2: So they’re incomplete in that respect,
translation_text.an10.10:1.4: ‘How can I become faithful and ethical?’
translation_text.an10.10:1.5: When the mendicant is faithful and ethical, they’re complete in that respect.
translation_text.an10.10:2.1: A mendicant is faithful and ethical, but not learned …
translation_text.an10.10:2.4: they don’t teach Dhamma to the assembly with assurance …
translation_text.an10.10:2.5: they’re not an expert in the monastic law …
translation_text.an10.10:2.6: they don’t recollect their many kinds of past lives …
translation_text.an10.10:2.7: they don’t, with clairvoyance that is purified and superhuman, see sentient beings passing away and being reborn …
translation_text.an10.10:2.8: they don’t realize the undefiled freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom in this very life, and live having realized it with their own insight due to the ending of defilements.
translation_text.an10.10:2.9: So they’re incomplete in that respect,
translation_text.an10.10:2.11: ‘How can I become faithful, ethical, and learned, a Dhamma speaker, one who frequents assemblies, one who teaches Dhamma to the assembly with assurance, an expert in the training, one who recollects their many kinds of past lives, one who with clairvoyance that surpasses the human sees sentient beings passing away and being reborn, and one who lives having realized the ending of defilements?’
translation_text.an10.10:3.1: When they are faithful, ethical, and learned, a Dhamma speaker, one who frequents assemblies, one who teaches Dhamma to the assembly with assurance, an expert in the training, one who recollects their many kinds of past lives, one who with clairvoyance that surpasses the human sees sentient beings passing away and being reborn, and one who lives having realized the ending of defilements,
translation_text.an10.10:3.3: A mendicant who possesses these ten qualities is impressive all around, and is complete in every respect.”
